design_philosophy.core_loop: Twin-stick arena shooter: eliminate waves of enemies entering from four edges, collect money/prizes/power-ups, choose exit to next arena, repeat until boss encounter
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Crowd control under pressure - managing multiple enemy waves simultaneously while maximizing resource collection in confined single-screen spaces
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Contestant on a violent, high-stakes dystopian game show competing for cash, prizes, and survival glory in increasingly chaotic arenas
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Embraces arcade-era coin-op design philosophy: high difficulty by design, player progression gated by skill/replays, rewards collection/greed over pure survival, dual-joystick control scheme demands constant directional awareness, cooperative competition (shared level but personal reward tracking)

difficulty.lives_system: Single-player and cooperative multi-stage progression with arcade continues model
difficulty.continues_system: Arcade game designed around continue-feeding economy; home ports preserve difficulty curve
difficulty.checkpointing: Linear arena progression per level - no backtracking allowed; map reveals boss location after first arena clear; progression is one-way forward
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Hidden level select cheat code available in SNES version (Right, Right, Up, Down, R, L at player select) allows skipping directly to any level or boss, suggesting developer acknowledgment of difficulty gatekeeping

progression.upgrade_system: Temporary power-ups and weapons collected mid-arena; permanent progression through money/prize collection for scoring; key collection unlocks bonus Pleasure Dome level
progression.unlock_mechanism: Keys scattered throughout levels - sufficient collection triggers access to Pleasure Dome bonus arena; level progression enforced via boss defeat; hidden level select for sequence-breaking
replayability_hooks: Competitive score tracking in cooperative play (individual cash totals rewarded differently)
Key collection for Pleasure Dome access encourages thorough arena exploration
Multiple exit choices per arena create emergent path variety
Prize/money maximization encourages risk-taking during enemy waves
Turbo mode test screen suggests alternate difficulty/speed configurations

world_building_techniques: Game show framing device (announcer voice, host character)
Prize economy mimics 1980s consumer goods (VCRs, toasters) establishing temporal setting
Dystopian 1999 future setting references RoboCop and The Running Man (1987)
Host verbal interjections punctuate gameplay ('Total carnage! I love it!', 'Big money! Big prizes!')
Final boss is the sadistic host character, personifying game show authority
Pleasure Dome bonus level commodifies female bodies as 'prizes' alongside appliances, establishing satire of game show excess

boss_design.philosophy: End-of-level gate requiring survival of previous arenas; sadistic host serves as final boss; boss placement confirmed via map display after first arena completion
notable_bosses: Sadistic game show host (final boss, participates in show narrative)

faction_indicators: Game show contestant role (players vs. hosts/systems)
Cooperative vs. competitive dynamics (shared progression, individual scoring)
danger_signals: Wave-based enemy spawning creates constant threat escalation
Money/prize collection as primary incentive creates risk-reward tension
No retreat mechanic enforces forward momentum into danger
Boss gatekeeping prevents level progression without demonstrated skill
environmental_storytelling: Four-edge arena design suggests enclosed game show set
Enemy wave patterns create rhythm of danger/collection cycles
Exit choices create moment of agency within predetermined progression
Prize visual design (bikini-clad 'babes' as collectibles) critiques game show exploitation

sources_used: wikipedia - Smash TV (comprehensive gameplay, development, design context, dystopian themes, boss narrative)
tcrf - Super Smash T.V. (SNES) (technical cheat codes including level select, hidden features, regional differences)
